text.title: ES Projections Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules grounding event-sourced projection design.

rule.statement: Projection handlers MUST be idempotent. Apply the same event twice MUST produce the same read-model state. Use UPSERT keyed by `(stream_id, position)` or `event_id`, never raw INSERT.
rule.rationale: Event subscriptions can redeliver on restart/reconnect; non-idempotent inserts produce duplicate rows or constraint failures.

rule.statement: Every projection MUST track its last processed `(stream_id, position)` (or global sequence number) in a `projection_checkpoint` table. On startup it MUST resume from that checkpoint.
rule.rationale: Without a checkpoint, restart replays from offset 0 every time, or skips events; neither is acceptable in production.

rule.statement: Projection handlers MUST only write to the read store (UPSERT / INSERT / DELETE). They MUST NOT send email, call other services, trigger workflows, or emit domain events. Side effects belong in process managers / sagas subscribed to the same events.
rule.rationale: Rebuilds replay every event from offset 0; side effects would fire each rebuild (duplicate emails, duplicate charges).

rule.statement: Every projection MUST be rebuildable by truncating its read table + checkpoint, then replaying events from offset 0. A `rebuild-<name>` command MUST exist and produce a read model byte-for-byte equivalent to the incrementally-built one.
rule.rationale: Without a rebuild path, the read model is permanently coupled to its history; bug fixes can't be back-applied.

rule.statement: The rebuild path MUST be exercised at least quarterly (CI job or manual rehearsal). Drift between incremental + rebuild output MUST fail the rehearsal.
rule.rationale: Unexercised rebuild paths rot — schema changes break them silently. Periodic test is the only reliable detector.
